tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Which type of workflow is shown in the image?

dialogs.thought: Identify the figure type and clinical context for 'staging' (likely cancer staging workflow).

content.content: A black-and-white flowchart titled "Staging" showing a clinical decision pathway with boxes listing standard tests (e.g., digital rectal exam, PSA), imaging options (CT, MRI, bone scan), and a classification step mentioning TNM staging, with branches to different stage categories (T1/T2, T3, N1).

dialogs.thought: Extract any explicit definitions or key phrases in the diagram about staging and classification.

arguments.attribute: what the classification box indicates about staging

dialogs.thought: Confirm that staging culminates in TNM classification and stage grouping, supporting the definition/purpose.

content.content: The region shows a box labeled "Classification" followed by "Standard: TNM staging," indicating that staging is used to classify the cancer by TNM criteria (tumor, nodes, metastasis).

dialogs.content: The image shows a cancer staging workflow. This is because the figure is organized as a clinical decision flowchart titled "Staging," and it includes the standard tests, imaging studies, and classification steps used to determine how advanced a cancer is. tem used in cancer staging.
